title: Other metals and minerals
gist: A thin Glencore corporate landing page describing its alumina/aluminium and iron ore marketing business. The only hard signal is 2025 marketing volumes: 11.4 mt of alumina/aluminium and 95.4 mt of iron ore sold via its marketing arm, sourced from third-party producers with Asia as the main iron ore customer base. Light card — promotional/factsheet fragment, not analysis.

insights.claim: Glencore's marketing business sold 95.4 mt of iron ore and 11.4 mt of alumina/aluminium in 2025, sourced from third-party producers rather than its own mines.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Sizes Glencore's third-party trading footprint in bulk ferrous/aluminium markets; iron ore at ~95 mt makes it a meaningful merchant flow to watch for margin and volume disclosures in results.
insights.quote: 95.4 mtof iron ore sold via our marketing business

insights.claim: Glencore physically markets iron ore from third-party producers with Asia making up most of its customer base, tying its iron ore flows to Asian (chiefly steel) demand.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Glencore's iron ore marketing is leveraged to Asian steel demand, so the volume is a read-through on regional steelmaking activity rather than on Glencore's own mining.
insights.quote: We physically market iron ore from third-party producers to customers worldwide – with Asia making up most of our customer base.
